defn.description: Defines a named function in the current namespace. The first argument is the function name, followed by an optional docstring, a vector of parameter bindings, and the function body. Supports multi-arity definitions with multiple parameter vectors and bodies wrapped in parentheses.

def.description: Creates a global Var in the current namespace bound to the given value. Vars are the primary mechanism for defining top-level bindings. Re-evaluating def replaces the root binding, which is visible to all threads unless thread-local bindings exist.

let.description: Creates local bindings in a lexical scope. Takes a vector of binding pairs (name value) and a body of expressions. Bindings are sequential—later bindings can reference earlier ones. The value of the last expression in the body is returned.

fn.description: Creates an anonymous (lambda) function. Takes a vector of parameters and a body of expressions. Supports multi-arity definitions and can optionally include a name for self-reference in recursive calls. Used extensively for callbacks, map functions, and closures.

map.description: Returns a lazy sequence consisting of the result of applying function f to each item in the collection. When passed multiple collections, applies f to corresponding items from each, stopping when the shortest collection is exhausted. One of the most fundamental higher-order functions in Clojure.

filter.description: Returns a lazy sequence of the items in the collection for which the predicate function returns a logically true value. Items for which the predicate returns nil or false are excluded from the result. Can also be used as a transducer when called with only a predicate.

reduce.description: Reduces the collection to a single value by repeatedly applying function f to an accumulator and each element. If init is supplied, it serves as the initial accumulator; otherwise the first two elements are used. Returns the final accumulated value after processing all elements.

atom.description: Creates an Atom, a thread-safe mutable reference type that provides synchronous, uncoordinated, independent state management. Atoms ensure atomic updates via compare-and-swap semantics and are the primary way to manage shared mutable state in Clojure programs.

deref.description: Returns the current value held by the atom without blocking or modifying it.

swap!.description: Atomically updates the atom's value by applying function f to the current value and any additional args, retrying if another thread modified it concurrently.

reset!.description: Sets the atom's value to new-value without regard to the current value, returning the new value.

compare-and-set!.description: Atomically sets the atom to newval if and only if the current value is identical to oldval, returning true on success.

add-watch.description: Adds a watch function that is called whenever the atom's value changes, receiving the key, atom, old value, and new value.

str.description: Coerces its arguments to strings and concatenates them. With no args returns the empty string. With one arg, returns its toString value. With multiple args, returns the concatenation of all toString values. nil arguments are treated as empty strings.

println.description: Prints the string representation of each argument separated by spaces, followed by a newline, to the current value of *out* (typically standard output). Uses pr-str for string conversion, which prints strings without surrounding quotes.
